# dating.fit_poisson_spline { #tslai.dating.fit_poisson_spline }

```python
tslai.dating.fit_poisson_spline(
    centers,
    events,
    exposure,
    lam,
    n_knots=25,
    degree=3,
    order=2,
    max_iter=60,
    tol=1e-07,
)
```

Penalised Poisson log-spline via penalised IRLS.

Model ``events ~ Poisson(exposure · exp(B β))`` with 2nd-difference penalty ``λ βᵀPβ``.

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[**rate**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [(n_cells,) ndarray]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Fitted rate ``exp(Bβ)`` at the cell centres.

<code>[**edf**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Effective degrees of freedom (trace of the smoother hat matrix) — for GCV.

<code>[**beta**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [(ncoef,) ndarray]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:
